message:
test+module(ruby): expand archive-extraction coverage to Pathname + Minitar idioms

Follow-up to the 5.1.36 ruby release that added the archive-
extraction path-traversal check. The first cut of the scanner
covered the dominant `Zip::File` / `Gem::Package::TarReader` shapes
but missed three Ruby idioms that production code (and a careful
test-fixture audit) surface as common-enough-to-care-about
extraction paths:

  - **Pathname-arithmetic builds.** `Pathname.new(destination) + entry.path`
    is the idiomatic way to build a child path in modern Ruby (and
    in Rails). The previous regex only spotted `File.join` /
    `File.expand_path` shapes; the `Pathname.new + entry.path`
    shape didn't fire.
  - **Minitar reader.** `Minitar::Reader.open(...)` is the
    standard-library-adjacent tar reader used by `bundler` and
    several CLI gems. The original `ARCHIVE_HINT_RE` only listed
    `Gem::Package::TarReader`; minitar uses entirely different
    constants, so its extraction sites were silently invisible to
    the scanner.
  - **`.path` accessor on archive entries.** rubyzip's `Zip::Entry`
    exposes both `.name` and `.path` (the former is the raw
    archive name, the latter the same value normalized for
    filesystem use). The original `ENTRY_NAME_RE` matched
    `.name` / `.full_name` but not `.path`, so any code that read
    `entry.path` for the target name went undetected.

Module updates:

  - `ARCHIVE_HINT_RE` adds `Minitar::Reader`, `Archive::Reader` (a
    common typedef alias used by some Bundler internals).
  - `ENTRY_NAME_RE` adds `.path` alongside `.name` and `.full_name`.
  - `ALIAS_ASSIGN_RE` mirrors the same `.path` addition so
    `target_name = entry.path` style intermediates also flow into
    the scan.
  - `PATH_BUILD_RE` gains:
      * `Pathname.new\s*\(` to spot the Pathname-arithmetic shape
      * `\+\s*(File::SEPARATOR|"...")` and the mirror form to spot
        explicit string-concatenation paths
      * `#\{...\}[^"']*/` and the mirror form to spot string
        interpolation paths (rare but real)

Fixture updates:

  - `archive_extraction_buggy/zip_slip.rb` adds an
    `unzip_with_pathname_unsafe` function exercising
    `Pathname.new(destination) + entry.path` →
    `File.binwrite(target.to_s, ...)`. The fixture's expectation
    is that it triggers the new finding; the existing
    `unzip_unsafe` and `untar_unsafe` functions still cover the
    File.join / File.binwrite shapes.
  - `archive_extraction_clean/zip_slip_safe.rb` adds a matching
    `unzip_with_pathname_safe` that uses `Pathname.new(destination)
    + entry.path` followed by `target.cleanpath.to_s.start_with?(...)`
    rejection. Locks the suppression heuristic on the new shape.
  - `test-suite/ruby/README.md` row text updated to mention the
    new shapes ("RubyZip/TarReader/**Minitar**") and the new
    suppressor idiom ("`File.expand_path`/**Pathname** +
    `start_with?` containment checks").

No release plumbing changes — this is in-place hardening of the
5.1.36 release's check, not a 5.1.37 bump. The next release-
worthy increment is the C# port queued separately.

files.patch:
@@ -534,18 +534,23 @@ BASE_DIR = ROOT if ROOT.is_dir() else ROOT.parent
 SKIP_DIRS = {'.git', '.bundle', 'vendor', 'node_modules', 'tmp', 'log', 'coverage', '.cache', 'dist', 'build'}
 EXTS = {'.rb', '.rake', '.ru', '.gemspec', '.erb', '.haml', '.slim', '.rbi', '.rbs', '.jbuilder'}
 ARCHIVE_HINT_RE = re.compile(
-    r'\b(?:Zip::File|Zip::InputStream|Gem::Package::TarReader|Archive::Tar|Zlib::GzipReader)\b'
+    r'\b(?:Zip::File|Zip::InputStream|Gem::Package::TarReader|'
+    r'Minitar::Reader|Archive::Tar|Archive::Reader|Zlib::GzipReader)\b'
 )
-ENTRY_NAME_RE = re.compile(r'\b[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*\.(?:name|full_name)\b')
+ENTRY_NAME_RE = re.compile(r'\b[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*\.(?:name|full_name|path)\b')
 ALIAS_ASSIGN_RE = re.compile(
     r'\b([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*'
-    r'[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*\.(?:name|full_name)\b'
+    r'[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*\.(?:name|full_name|path)\b'
 )
 PATH_BUILD_RE = re.compile(
     r'\bFile\.(?:join|expand_path|write|open|binwrite)\s*\(|'
+    r'\bPathname\.new\s*\(|'
     r'\bFileUtils\.(?:mkdir_p|cp|mv|touch)\s*\(|'
     r'\.extract\s*\(|'
-    r'\.join\s*\('
+    r'\.join\s*\(|'
+    r'\+\s*(?:File::SEPARATOR|["\'][^"\']*/[^"\']*["\'])|'
+    r'(?:File::SEPARATOR|["\'][^"\']*/[^"\']*["\'])\s*\+|'
+    r'#\{[^}]+\}[^"\']*/|/[^"\']*#\{[^}]+\}'
 )
 SAFE_NAMED_RE = re.compile(
     r'\b(?:safe_destination|safe_archive_path|safe_zip_entry|safe_entry_path|'

@@ -6,8 +6,8 @@
 | `buggy/resource_lifecycle.rb` | File/thread cleanup |
 | `buggy/buggy_scripts.rb` | Shelling out, missing rescue |
 | `buggy/performance.rb` | Thread leaks, backticks |
-| `archive_extraction_buggy/zip_slip.rb` | RubyZip/TarReader path traversal |
-| `archive_extraction_clean/zip_slip_safe.rb` | `File.expand_path` + `start_with?` containment checks |
+| `archive_extraction_buggy/zip_slip.rb` | RubyZip/TarReader/Minitar path traversal |
+| `archive_extraction_clean/zip_slip_safe.rb` | `File.expand_path`/`Pathname` + `start_with?` containment checks |
 | Clean files | Managed threads, Open3 argv |
 
 ```bash

@@ -1,4 +1,5 @@
 require "fileutils"
+require "pathname"
 require "zip"
 require "rubygems/package"
 
@@ -20,3 +21,30 @@ def untar_unsafe(io, destination)
     File.binwrite(target, entry.read)
   end
 end
+
+def unzip_with_pathname_unsafe(archive_path, destination)
+  Zip::InputStream.open(archive_path) do |zip_stream|
+    while (entry = zip_stream.get_next_entry)
+      target = Pathname.new(destination) + entry.path
+      FileUtils.mkdir_p(target.dirname)
+      File.binwrite(target.to_s, zip_stream.read)
+    end
+  end
+end
+
+def untar_with_string_concat_unsafe(io, destination)
+  Minitar::Reader.open(io).each do |entry|
+    target = destination + "/" + entry.full_name
+    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(target))
+    File.binwrite(target, entry.read)
+  end
+end
+
+def untar_with_interpolation_unsafe(io, destination)
+  Archive::Reader.open(io).each do |entry|
+    name = entry.path
+    target = "#{destination}/#{name}"
+    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(target))
+    File.binwrite(target, entry.read)
+  end
+end

@@ -1,4 +1,5 @@
 require "fileutils"
+require "pathname"
 require "zip"
 require "rubygems/package"
 
@@ -32,3 +33,25 @@ def untar_safely(io, destination)
     File.binwrite(target, entry.read)
   end
 end
+
+def untar_with_pathname_safely(io, destination)
+  base = Pathname.new(destination).realpath
+  Minitar::Reader.open(io).each do |entry|
+    target = (Pathname.new(base.to_s) + entry.full_name).cleanpath
+    unless target.to_s.start_with?(base.to_s + File::SEPARATOR) || target == base
+      raise ArgumentError, "archive entry escapes destination"
+    end
+    FileUtils.mkdir_p(target.dirname)
+    File.binwrite(target.to_s, entry.read)
+  end
+end
+
+def unzip_with_safe_interpolation(archive_path, destination)
+  Zip::InputStream.open(archive_path) do |zip_stream|
+    while (entry = zip_stream.get_next_entry)
+      target = safe_archive_path(destination, entry.path)
+      FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(target))
+      File.binwrite(target, zip_stream.read)
+    end
+  end
+end
